farmOS documentation: https://v1.farmos.org/guide/logs/ confirms Activity is a primary log type

farmOS documentation: https://farmos.org/guide/logs/ confirms Input logs are used for resources applied to assets
farmOS documentation: https://farmos.org/guide/logs/ confirms Observation is used for passive farm observations
Counted from required_logs array in task.json and verifier.py REQUIRED_LOGS list
Verified in verifier.py line 35: PASS_THRESHOLD = 80 (4 of 5 logs × 20 points = 80 points)
Pioneer research: https://www.pioneer.com/us/agronomy/corn-plant-population-research-cropfocus.html confirms optimal range 30000-38500 plants/acre; 32500 is within recommended range
Pioneer official documentation: https://www.firstseedtests.com/products/corn/pioneer-p1197am/ confirms P1197AM is a real CRM 111 hybrid with AM, LL, RR2 traits

pi_items.verified_value: 32 oz/ac = 1.0 lb a.e./ac (assuming Roundup PowerMax 4 lbs a.e./gal standard formulation)
pi_items.source: Iowa State Extension: https://crops.extension.iastate.edu/post/cover-crop-termination-review-2023 recommends 0.63-1.0 lb a.e./ac for winter rye; 32 oz/ac is at upper end of recommended range

K-State and University of Minnesota: https://eupdate.agronomy.ksu.edu/article_new/starter-fertilizer-rates-and-placement-for-corn-554-4 confirms 5 gal/ac is standard safe rate for in-furrow 10-34-0 on corn

EOS Precision Agriculture and UGA: https://eos.com/blog/grid-soil-sampling/ and https://fieldreport.caes.uga.edu/publications/C1297/ confirm 2.5-acre grids are industry standard for precision sampling
Crop Quest and Ohio State: https://geopard.tech/blog/grid-soil-sampling-remote-sensing/ confirms industry practice is 5-15 cores per grid; 8 cores is well within range

Precision Planting research: https://www.precisionplanting.com/resources/research/corn-singulation-study confirms target singulation is 99.5%, typical range 98-99.6%; 99.2% is realistic field performance slightly below ideal

pi_items.verified_value: 7-14 days typical, but 3-6 weeks for allelopathy concerns in split termination scenarios
pi_items.source: Ohio State and MSU: https://agcrops.osu.edu/newsletter/corn-newsletter/2020-05/cover-crop-termination and https://www.canr.msu.edu/resources/cover-crop-termination confirm 7-14 day minimum, but 3+ weeks noted for complete decomposition and allelopathy reduction

pi_items.verified_value: 2 days after lab receipt (normal turnaround per Midwest Labs documentation)
pi_items.source: Midwest Labs FAQ: https://midwestlabs.com/get-started/helpful-faqs states 'normal turnaround on soils and plants is 2 days after the sample is received' with most samples completed end-to-end in less than 3 days

privileged_info_summary: This is a legitimate agricultural task for the farmOS Field Kit Android app. All log types (Activity, Input, Observation) are verified as real farmOS log types. The agricultural practices described are evidence-based and realistic: corn population of 32,500 seeds/acre is within the optimal 30,000-38,500 range; Pioneer P1197AM is a verified real hybrid (CRM 111); glyphosate at 32 oz/ac equals 1.0 lb a.e./ac, within the 0.63-1.0 recommended range for winter rye cover crop termination; 10-34-0 starter fertilizer at 5 gal/ac is the standard safe in-furrow rate; 2.5-acre grid soil sampling with 8 cores per grid is industry standard practice; and 99.2% seed singulation accuracy is realistic field performance. The pass threshold of 80 points (4 of 5 logs) is correctly calculated from 20 points per log. One claim is contradicted: soil test turnaround time is stated as 5 business days but Midwest Labs documentation specifies 2 days after sample receipt. The task data is not synthetic but rather user-generated through interactions with the real farmOS Field Kit app.
